title: Gomphandra pallida
taxonomy: Gomphandra pallida is a species of flowering plant in the family Stemonuraceae. It is native to Southeast Asia and belongs to the genus Gomphandra. Gomphandra pallida is an evergreen tree that is commonly found in the tropical forests of the region. The plant is classified as a dicotyledonous species, meaning it has two cotyledons or seed leaves in its embryonic leaves. This classification is based on the plant's botanical characteristics and its evolutionary relationships with other plant species.
uses: Gomphandra pallida is reported to have various medicinal uses, including treating fever and respiratory issues. Its roots and leaves are used in traditional medicine in some regions, particularly in Asia. The plant is believed to have antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to its potential health benefits. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal properties and effects on human health. Studies suggest that Gomphandra pallida may have anti-inflammatory properties, which could be beneficial for treating certain conditions.

intro: Gomphandra pallida, also known as the 'Pallid Hopia', is a species of flowering plant in the family Stemonuraceae, native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. The bioactive constituents of Gomphandra pallida include glycosides, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or tea, or used to make a tincture, which is used to treat a range of health conditions.
